Annotation.String: Terms for providing AI-specific guidance on API resources
2026-06-05 © Copyright 2026 SAP SE. All rights reserved

Term.Name: hint
Term.AppliesTo: EntityType EntitySet NavigationProperty Property EntityContainer TypeDefinition

Annotation.String: A free-text hint for AI consumers (e.g., large language models or AI agents) on how to use or interpret the annotated element.

Provides AI-specific guidance on how to use or interpret the annotated OData element. It is intentionally kept separate from human-readable descriptions (e.g., Core.Description) so that end-user-facing documentation and AI-targeted guidance can evolve independently.
The annotation value is a plain string, optionally formatted using lightweight Markdown (bold labels, bullet points, inline code). Typical content includes:
* Business context: what business concept or domain object the element represents * Usage guidance: when and how to use this entity, property, or service * Disambiguation: how to distinguish from similar entities or operations * Value constraints: valid values, formats, coding standards (e.g., ISO codes, enums) * Authorization: required scopes or roles for write operations (service level) * Routing guidance: when NOT to use this element and what to use instead
The content of AI.hint is not intended for display to end users and MUST be filtered when publishing metadata externally (e.g., to public API catalogs).
The corresponding annotation in ABAP CDS / CSN Interop is @AI.hint. In JSON Schema-based formats (git@github.com:vyshnavigadamsetti/odata-vocabularies.gitOpenAPI, AsyncAPI), the equivalent is x-sap-ai-hint.
